project.name: trustanchor
project.currentFocus: Phase 8 Security Hardening is next after completing Enhanced Verification Phase 7.
durableFacts: Docker development image installs Node dependencies with npm ci during build, not at container startup.
The web service host port is configurable through WEB_PORT with default 3000.
Bootstrap admin authentication is environment-backed through BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME and BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD.
BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD may be plaintext in development/test and must be SHA-256 hex in production.
The active UI concept is a forensic document light table with layered evidence sheets, registration rails, and CSS-only inspection-light motion.
Admin routes are protected by Next.js middleware using JWT session tokens stored in cookies.
Document proof signing now uses RSA-PSS SHA-256 (PS256) to align with the cryptography assignment rubric.
Public verification pages render scannable QR codes from qrcode-generator.
Print-ready certificate pages are available at /verify/{verificationCode}/print.
Tampered or legacy proof material returns proofVerified false with status proof_invalid instead of a 500.
Bootstrap admin password accepts plaintext in development/test and requires SHA-256 hex in production.
Server-side certificate PDF rendering is available at /api/verifications/{verificationCode}/certificate-pdf and stores artifacts in MinIO.

lastAchievements: Redesigned landing, login, not-found, and verification result UI around the forensic light-table concept.
Implemented Admin Dashboard overview, template list, issuance list, and audit trail views.
Added forms for template and certificate issuance with strict zod validation and JSON parsing.
Fixed client-side Next.js App Router redirect caching issues on login using hard window.location.href navigation.
Updated .env and authentication.service to use SHA-256 password hashing instead of plain text.
Updated Roadmap to show Phase 5 and Phase 6 as COMPLETED (60% total progress).
Added cryptography assignment fit documentation mapping makalah, program, presentation, signer flow, verifier flow, and tamper demo evidence.
Started Phase 7 by adding QR code rendering on public verification pages.
Added QR SVG download endpoint and print-ready certificate artifact page.
Updated verification service to return a safe invalid-proof result when stored proof cannot validate.
Reworked the admin template form to collect academic certificate fields and generate layoutDefinition automatically.
Fixed local login with plaintext BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD while preserving production hash enforcement.
Removed Public Claims JSON from issuance creation and made public claims derive from the selected academic template.
Completed Phase 7 with server-side PDF rendering, MinIO artifact storage, and certificate revocation.
pendingIssues: Next.js 14.2.5 and npm audit reported security advisories during Docker build.
npm install reported 14 audit vulnerabilities after adding qrcode-generator, pdf-lib, and minio.
Playwright is not installed locally, so no browser screenshot regression was run.
.next cache is owned by nobody/nogroup, so lint must use --no-cache unless cache permissions are fixed.
nextBestActions: Begin Phase 8 with Redis-backed rate limiting and CSRF protection for admin mutations.
Upgrade Next.js to a patched compatible version in a separate dependency update.
validationState: npm run validate passed locally after adding the validate script.
API smoke test passed for health, OpenAPI, login, certificate template creation, certificate issuance, and public verification.
Document proof unit tests cover RSA-SHA256 happy path plus tampered hash and tampered encrypted payload rejection.
QR unit tests cover QR matrix and SVG generation.
Docker smoke passed for /verify/{code}, /verify/{code}/print, /api/verifications/{code}, and /api/verifications/{code}/qr using an existing local record.
Docker smoke passed for bootstrap login and academic template creation.
Docker end-to-end smoke passed for login, academic template creation, issuance without public claims JSON, PDF generation, MinIO object storage, revocation, and public verification status.
